assessments.dimension: renderer-tcb — the claim→Lean-Prop renderer as a load-bearing trusted artifact for the kernel bridge
key_risks: A renderer bug produces a kernel-checked Q.E.D. of a MIS-statement — promoting the renderer into the proof-edge TCB the project exists to protect.

'Renderer is faithful' is not kernel-checkable; it has no mechanical re-checker (unlike automaton-universality / ring / the kernel), so it rests permanently on human adversarial review against a reference semantics that must first be pinned.
Vacuous/weakened Prop laundering (dropped bound, ∀n→bounded, rendered negation, True) earns a real proof — the ADR 0020/0030 vacuous-PASS mode, now on the proof edge.

Decider↔renderer drift: the Walnut predicate and the Lean Prop are independent renderings of one claim and can denote different statements; without a round-trip/shared-IR check the kernel can certify a Prop that is not what Walnut decided.
Expressiveness pressure (custom DFAOs, modular arithmetic, unbounded ∀n for the strong targets) widens the trusted grammar exactly where review is hardest.
dependencies: A pinned machine-readable reference denotation of the ADR 0004 claim contract for the automatic-sequence class (does not exist yet).
A restricted, typed rendering grammar scoped to that first class (not a general claim→Prop function).
The kernel-bridge re-checker / Lean-kernel re-check path for certificate.kind='kernel-bridge' (ADR 0037 §2 run rung).
An adversarial-soundness-review process (ADR 0021/0030 precedent) extended to the proof edge, with known-mis-statement fixtures that must fail to promote.
A second rendering or shared IR enabling a round-trip agreement check between the Walnut decision and the Lean Prop.
An operator-signed ADR amendment authorizing renderer promotion into the proof-edge TCB.
assessments.rationale: The renderer is the single most trust-critical artifact in the kernel-bridge build and its cost is intrinsic, not a now-vs-later timing play. The merged seam (ADR 0037) and the Walnut backend genuinely de-risk the *engine* side — exact-or-DEFER, certificate + independent re-checker, OFF-BY-DEFAULT discipline, a concrete first target class — but they do NOT de-risk the renderer, because the renderer's hardness is semantic, not architectural. The kernel certifies that a proof of claim_prop is valid; it can NEVER certify that claim_prop *is* the claim. So the renderer is a trusted artifact with no mechanical re-checker — unlike every other certificate in the §2 table (automaton-universality, ring/SOS, the kernel itself), all of which re-derive a structural property the renderer alone has nothing analogous. That asymmetry is the crux: the bridge launders the renderer from the *faithfulness* TCB (where Walnut's walnut_predicate honestly lives today) into the *proof-edge* TCB — the exact boundary trust.py and test_invariants.py exist to defend, where a bug produces a real Q.E.D. on a mis-statement. The 'easier now' framing is mostly illusory: the seam helps wiring and the first target class narrows the grammar, but the load-bearing work — pinning a reference denotation for the claim contract, building a restricted typed grammar, and adversarially reviewing the renderer against attacks (vacuous-True, dropped bounds, rendered negation, decider/renderer drift) — is timing-independent and must happen whenever the bridge is built. Effort is L (not XL): the *narrow* automatic-sequence grammar plus reusing the ADR 0021/0030 review machinery is bounded and well-scoped, but the residual review burden is permanent and unfalsifiable (Walnut's structural re-checker still took three review rounds; a proof-edge renderer has weaker backstops). Recommended mitigations, in order of leverage: (1) FORMAL-FIRST publication — publish the Lean Prop + proof as the primary artifact so a human/reviewer reads the actual statement that was proved, never the informal claim, making mis-statement visible rather than hidden; (2) a RESTRICTED, TYPED rendering grammar per target class so the TCB is small and exhaustively reviewable; (3) a ROUND-TRIP / shared-IR check binding the Walnut decision and the Lean Prop to one denotation; (4) a known-mis-statement adversarial fixture set that must fail to promote, as a standing regression. Net: the bridge is buildable and the seam makes the surrounding plumbing cheap, but the renderer cost is real, intrinsic, and trust-critical — it should gate the run rung behind an explicit operator ADR amendment, and it is the strongest argument for first standing up a separate non-Q.E.D. Walnut-decided tier to MEASURE blind-novel yield before paying this trusted-artifact cost at all.

assessments.dimension: bridge-synthesis reachability (can Lean kernel + Goedel-V2/DeepSeek/Aristotle/repair-panel actually CLOSE the bridge goals for the automatic-sequence target class?)
key_risks: Near-total DEFER on the load-bearing goal shape. The bridge can produce two goal shapes, and they trade off: (A) the equivalence claim_prop <-> statement is often kernel-reachable (rfl/simp/decide), but when it is, it is trivial and proves nothing beyond 'the renderer restated the claim' -- no Q.E.D. of a real theorem; (B) a full Lean proof of the theorem itself (Tribonacci 4th-power-free over all i,p; Thue-Morse overlap-free; Stern record-setter language) is the valuable target, and it is the SAME unbounded-forall class that M2 found 0/24 expressible in the box -- precisely the class kernel-style provers DEFER on. Realistic DEFER rate on shape B for the 12 probe targets: ~10/12 to 12/12. Realistic 'first proved output' count: 0-2, and the 1-2 that close (e.g. a small Thue-Morse fact closable by decide over a finite reformulation) are textbook, not the blind-novel headlines.

Mathlib has none of the supporting theory. Confirmed by search: mathlib defines `catalan n` but has NO mod-2^k residue theorems; NO Stern diatomic sequence; NO Thue-Morse/Tribonacci/paperfolding/Fibonacci-word objects; NO overlap-free/power-free machinery; NO Buchi-Bruyere / automatic-sequence decidability. A prover must build the entire automaton-theoretic argument from first principles in Lean -- exactly what SOTA provers cannot do for research-paper combinatorics-on-words results (vs. miniF2F/competition problems where they score).

The bridge throws away Walnut's decision. Walnut DECIDES these via automaton synthesis over unbounded n (the Buchi-Bruyere procedure) -- the reason it reaches 12/12 where the box reaches 0/24. The Lean kernel has no analogue of that decision procedure, so asking a prover to close the theorem directly re-incurs the full hard proof Walnut was adopted to avoid. The decision and the kernel-checkable proof are NOT the same artifact; bridging is not a translation, it is a from-scratch re-proof.

Renderer TCB makes the cheap shape (A) the dangerous one. The reachable shape -- claim_prop <-> statement closed by rfl/simp -- is where a renderer bug yields a kernel-checked proof of a MIS-statement. The goals that ARE reachable are exactly the ones whose soundness rests entirely on the new trusted renderer, not on the kernel. So 'reachable' here correlates with 'trust-critical and low-value', the worst quadrant.

The strongest-novelty targets DEFER even in Walnut on engineering grounds (Motzkin mod 8, Gessel/Apery mod 8, odd-Catalan residue count need custom Rowland-Yassawi DFAOs per the probe). For those, the bridge has no decided certificate to bridge FROM, let alone a Lean proof of -- so they are out of reach at both layers, not just the kernel layer.
dependencies: A claim->Lean-Prop renderer for automatic-sequence claims (the new load-bearing TRUSTED artifact; ADR 0037 'run' rung Stage B). Without it there is no bridge goal to attempt.

A Lean formalization of each target sequence and the relevant predicate (Thue-Morse word, Tribonacci word over a numeration, Stern sequence, the overlap/power/factor predicates) -- absent from mathlib today, so either authored by hand (large) or none of shape B is even statable in Lean.

A working Lean 4.31 + Mathlib kernel path with the prover ensemble wired to attempt bridge goals (exists: LeanCliBackend Docker image + prover_ensemble + ProofRepairer/RepairingDemonstrate; this part is ready).
A decision on which goal shape the bridge targets (equivalence-only vs full theorem proof) -- the entire reachability verdict hinges on this and ADR 0037 does not pin it.

A bridge-synthesis reachability micro-probe (the M2-style measure-before-build gate ADR 0037 sec 4 mandates for the 'run' rung) -- NOT yet run; this assessment is the on-paper version of it and says it would likely come back RED for shape B.
assessments.rationale: Bridge-synthesis reachability for the first target class is poor, and it is poor for intrinsic reasons rather than missing infrastructure -- so it is harder NOW and does not get meaningfully easier by sequencing it after Walnut. The honest read: the bridge has two goal shapes with an inverse value/reachability relationship. The reachable shape (claim_prop <-> statement, closable by rfl/simp/decide) is trivial-by-construction and rests entirely on the new trusted renderer, so a Q.E.D. there certifies the renderer, not a theorem -- low value, high trust cost. The valuable shape (a full kernel proof of the unbounded-forall theorem) is exactly the class that (a) M2 showed the box cannot even state, (b) Walnut reaches only via an automaton-synthesis decision procedure the Lean kernel has no analogue of, and (c) mathlib has zero supporting theory for (confirmed: no Thue-Morse/Tribonacci/Stern objects, no power-freeness, no Buchi-Bruyere; only a bare `catalan` definition). SOTA provers Goedel-V2/DeepSeek/Aristotle score on competition-style goals with mathlib support, not on from-scratch research combinatorics-on-words proofs. Estimated DEFER rate on the 12 probe targets' theorem-shape goals: ~10-12 of 12, with 0-2 plausible closures and those being textbook, not the blind-novel headlines the project actually wants. The mechanism is the point: Walnut and the kernel reach these classes by fundamentally different means, and the bridge does not translate one into the other -- it demands a brand-new hard proof that discards Walnut's decision. So this is an M2-style RED on reachability: like the bounded box, the bridge would DEFER on ~everything that matters. The XL effort estimate is driven by the renderer TCB (full adversarial soundness review, like the Walnut three-round process) PLUS the from-scratch mathlib formalization of each sequence/predicate before a prover can even attempt shape B -- and after all that, the prover step is the part most likely to fail. The right move is the cheaper alternative the question names: stand up a separate NON-Q.E.D. Walnut-decided tier first, run the blind human panel on its output, and let proven blind-novelty be the trigger to invest in the bridge. Build the bridge only if (1) the blind panel confirms Walnut yields blind-novel results AND (2) a dedicated bridge-synthesis micro-probe returns better than this on-paper RED -- not before. Spending XL trust-critical effort to bridge a discovery channel not yet shown to discover anything is premature.

key_risks: The seam reduces the SCAFFOLDING (registration, dispatch, exact-or-DEFER, certificate plumbing, off-by-default safety, guard-test template) to near-zero, but gives essentially ZERO leverage on the two intrinsic hard parts: (1) the claim->Lean Prop renderer is a new load-bearing TRUSTED artifact, and (2) bridge-proof synthesis is undecidable -> wide DEFER. Mistaking the cheap scaffolding for a cheap build is the central risk.

Renderer-TCB mismatch: the seam's re-check pattern verifies a STRUCTURAL property of a certificate (Walnut: automaton universality), not that the rendered statement MEANS the claim. The Walnut backend explicitly punts this ('does NOT re-verify that the automaton is the agreement automaton for this claim'). The kernel re-derives the proof term but cannot certify the renderer; a renderer bug = a kernel-checked Q.E.D. of a MIS-statement -- precisely the catastrophe the trust invariants exist to prevent. The seam offers no help here.

Edge mismatch / outside-the-seam integration: Walnut and SOS land on the FAITHFULNESS edge; the kernel bridge is the only rung that intends to earn a PROOF edge / Q.E.D. The seam (FaithfulnessGate.sound_backends, recheckers) is built for the faithfulness edge. Routing a sound decision into the PROOF edge so kernel_verified/Q.E.D. can be set touches LeanVerifier.discharge's sole-writer position (the byte-identical trust core, tests/test_invariants.py). That integration is genuinely cold and is the highest-risk piece the seam does NOT cover.

Synthesis yield unknown: no existing automatic-sequence->Lean tactic; Mathlib automatic-sequence support is thin. The ADR 0029 repair panel + proposal models exist as drivers, but first-pass success rate on claim_prop<->statement bridges is unmeasured and likely low, so the bridge may DEFER on most real targets even after a clean build.
dependencies: The claim->Lean Prop RENDERER (new trusted artifact) -- the renderer must exist and pass an adversarial soundness review before any bridge proof can be trusted; this is the load-bearing prerequisite the seam does not provide.

A kernel-bridge certificate re-checker wrapping existing kernel-check infrastructure (LeanVerifier.discharge / backend.check_proof in verifiers.py:61 + lean_cli.py:147) -- reuse is high but it must be registered under the 'kernel-bridge' kind already named in Certificate (sound_backends.py:62).

A decision on WHICH edge the bridge feeds: faithfulness-edge-only (seam-native, low risk, but no Q.E.D.) vs proof-edge (Q.E.D.-earning, touches the discharge sole-writer + test_invariants.py). The seam only de-risks the former.

A concrete first target class already in hand: automatic-sequence theorems, with Walnut as the sound oracle and walnut_predicate/walnut_numeration rendering fields (propositio Expressio) reusable as structured source.
assessments.rationale: Net seam leverage is REAL but ASYMMETRIC: it collapses the integration scaffolding, not the build. Concrete delta vs cold: the protocol (sound_backends.py:90), the Certificate dataclass which ALREADY names 'kernel-bridge' as a kind (line 62), the re-checker registry (recheckers dict, faithfulness.py:73), the cost-ranked exact-or-DEFER dispatch with FAIL->quarantine and the authoritative 'rechecker must exist AND return True' PASS path (faithfulness.py:111-145), the off-by-default safety model, the 3-round adversarial-review template, and the generic guard-test harness (test_sound_backends_r0037.py) are ALL reusable as-is. A bridge backend is one applies/check class + one registered re-checker + a few guard tests -- the same shape Walnut already proved (Slices 1-2 merged). The kernel re-checker is the single biggest concrete win: LeanVerifier.discharge / backend.check_proof IS already a 're-verify a Lean proof term against a statement via the kernel' function, so the bridge wraps it rather than building a kernel checker. That is why the INTEGRATION specifically is M, not L: the faithfulness-edge wiring is genuinely cheap now and clearly cheaper than cold (cold would mean inventing the protocol, the certificate/re-check discipline, dispatch, and the safety model from scratch -- all sunk by Slices 1-2). BUT the two named costs are intrinsic and the seam touches neither: the renderer is a brand-new trusted artifact whose correctness the kernel CANNOT certify (the seam's re-check verifies certificate structure, not statement meaning), and synthesis is undecidable. If the bridge must earn Q.E.D. (proof edge), it also steps outside the seam into the discharge sole-writer / test_invariants core, which the seam was not built for. So: scaffolding is the cheap part and the seam makes it cheap NOW; the hard parts (renderer trust, synthesis yield, proof-edge routing) are unchanged. Honest verdict for the integration dimension alone: M, easier now (the patterns + a concrete automatic-sequence target + Walnut-as-oracle are in hand and decay if rebuilt cold), but do not let M-on-integration be read as M-on-the-whole-bridge -- the renderer and synthesis are separately L/XL and are where the trust-critical risk lives.

key_risks: INTRINSIC-COST TRAP: the two headline costs do not drop with warm context. (i) The claim->Lean-Prop renderer is a new load-bearing TRUSTED artifact (renderer bug = a kernel-checked proof of a MIS-statement, defeating the project's entire reason to exist); its soundness is a property of the artifact, not of when it is built. (ii) Bridge synthesis (claim_prop <-> statement) is UNDECIDABLE in general -> a structurally WIDE DEFER that no amount of warm context narrows. Building now buys nothing against either; it only pays the seam-discount on the cheap parts.

MEASUREMENT GAP (the decisive risk): every prior rung measured 0 blind-novel (ADR 0034 richer proposer, ADR 0035 richer grammar, ADR 0036 M1/M2). The Walnut probe is GREEN on REACHABILITY + SOUNDNESS only and EXPLICITLY 0 on novelty ('GREEN means the paradigm is worth building, not that Walnut discovers'; structured novelty field has 0 research tags). Committing the XL trust-critical bridge before a single blind-novel Walnut result exists risks building the hardest, most dangerous rung to mint Q.E.D. certificates for the textbook genre M1 already showed is the floor.

MATHLIB FORMALIZATION DEBT: stating claim_prop for k-automatic sequences requires Mathlib's automatic-sequence / Buchi-Bruyere formalization to be mature enough to even express the target class. If it is thin, the renderer must itself define the semantics in Lean -- enlarging the trusted artifact precisely where bugs are silent. This is an external dependency whose maturity is timing-independent and outside the project's control.

SEAM-MISFIT: the existing re-checker registry honestly re-checks a STRUCTURAL property (automaton universality) with Walnut left in the faithfulness TCB. The kernel-bridge re-check is a DIFFERENT, STRONGER shape (full kernel re-derivation, per ADR 0037 table row 'Run'). The seam admits it as a registration, but the bridge does NOT inherit the Walnut re-checker's lighter discipline -- the heavy review (ADR 0037 sec 4: 'the heaviest review, last') is unavoidable and does not shrink by being adjacent in time.

OPTION-VALUE FORFEIT if built now: a Walnut decision currently has NOWHERE TO LAND (it produces a faithfulness verdict, not a Codex entry). A cheap non-Q.E.D. 'decided-by-Walnut' tier (reuse quarantine/FinishReason + the reading-room, no new TCB) would let the unchanged blind human panel MEASURE blind-novelty for ~S-M effort. Skipping it to go straight to the bridge forfeits the cheap kill-signal and bets XL trust-critical effort on an unproven discovery hypothesis.
dependencies: A non-Q.E.D. Walnut-decided tier (the measurement rung): wire the OFF-BY-DEFAULT WalnutBackend into a live path that emits a DECIDED-but-not-promulgated record (reuse Propositio.quarantine / FinishReason + the Calculemus reading-room), so decisions land somewhere a blind panel can read them. ~S-M, no new TCB surface, no trust-boundary change.

A blind human-panel novelty read (ADR 0034 sec 5, unchanged) on Walnut output >= 1 blind-novel result -- the trigger that converts 'paradigm worth building' into 'discovery worth minting Q.E.D. for'. Without this, the bridge has no demonstrated payload.

Operator authorization to run the sandbox-blocked external Java (Walnut) -- the probe noted the real run is sandbox-blocked and awaits authorization; no live Walnut decisions exist yet, so there is literally nothing to bridge today.

A claim->Lean-Prop RENDERER for automatic-sequence theorems (does NOT exist; the walnut_predicate renderer targets Walnut's command language, a different artifact) + sufficient Mathlib k-automatic-sequence formalization to state claim_prop -- the new trusted artifact, gated behind its own reachability probe (ADR 0037 sec 4 run-rung discipline) and an adversarial soundness review.
Bridge-proof synthesis via existing provers (Goedel-Prover-V2 / Aristotle / DeepSeek-Prover + the ADR 0029 repair panel), with explicit DEFER accounting for the undecidable-in-general fraction.
assessments.rationale: VERDICT: LATER -- and the timing economics are unusually clean here. The kernel-bridge cost decomposes into (a) a small seam-shaped part that IS cheaper now (warm context: the SoundFaithfulnessBackend protocol, re-checker registry, certificate pattern, Expressio fields, and three rounds of soundness-review muscle memory all exist and would be re-learned if deferred), and (b) the two HARD parts that are fully INTRINSIC and timing-invariant: the renderer-as-trusted-artifact (a soundness property of the artifact, identical whenever built) and undecidable bridge synthesis (a fixed mathematical fact). Crucially the warm-context discount applies ONLY to part (a), which is the cheap part; it does NOT touch the XL cost or the trust risk, which live entirely in part (b). So 'build now to capture warm context' is a real but SMALL saving on the part that was never the constraint -- a classic intrinsic-cost trap. Against that small saving sits a large, asymmetric measurement option. Today a Walnut decision has nowhere to land and -- per the ADR 0037 sec 6 GREEN finding's own honest bound and the converging 0-blind-novel results of ADR 0034/0035/0036 -- there is ZERO evidence Walnut produces blind-novel output rather than the textbook floor M1 identified. A cheap non-Q.E.D. Walnut-decided tier (reuse quarantine/reading-room, ~S-M, no new TCB, trust boundary untouched) buys a decisive de-risking signal: if the blind panel reads 0 blind-novel, the XL trust-critical bridge is correctly NOT built and a renderer-bug-shaped catastrophe is never risked; if it reads >=1, the bridge is now justified AND newly cheap to scope because we know the concrete target class and have validated payloads. Measuring FIRST de-risks rather than wastes the bridge work: nothing in the measurement tier is throwaway (the seam, backend, and reading-room are reused unchanged), and the renderer/synthesis -- the only genuinely new artifacts -- are exactly what we should NOT build speculatively, because their cost is intrinsic and their soundness review is the heaviest in the project. This also matches ADR 0037 sec 4's own staging ('Run/kernel bridge ... last, heaviest review') and the project's binding constraint being NOVELTY, not prover reach. RIGHT TRIGGER: build the bridge when (1) the cheap tier yields >=1 blind-panel-confirmed blind-novel Walnut decision over unbounded n, AND (2) a run-rung reachability probe confirms the target class is renderable into a Mathlib Prop with a re-checkable bridge proof for a non-trivial fraction (wide DEFER expected and acceptable). Until both fire, standing up the bridge spends the project's scarcest resource (trust-critical review bandwidth) to mint Q.E.D. for output that may not exist.

key_risks: Renderer joins the TCB as a load-bearing trusted artifact whose failure mode (kernel-checked proof of a mis-statement) is the exact 'strictly worse than no proof' failure the faithfulness gate exists to prevent — and the kernel re-check certifies proof<->Prop, never Prop<->Enuntiatio, so the renderer sits in an unguarded, mechanically-uncheckable gap.

The bridge's 'strongest rung' framing (full kernel re-derivation) covers only the proof TERM; it silently promotes a TRUSTED step (rendering) onto the proof edge, so the renderer is a residual the kernel does not see despite the rung being sold as the most-derived.

Q.E.D./invariants stay honest ONLY IF the bridge is a genuine Lean proof through the existing discharge/check_proof; any shortcut that sets kernel_verified outside discharge or stamps the proof edge with a non-KERNEL producer breaks the boundary (validate_edge rejects it, so it would surface — but it's the design trap to avoid).

Review burden exceeds Walnut's 3 rounds: the renderer's correctness is a semantic faithfulness property with no mechanical oracle (the 3-body problem itself), so expect 4-5 adversarial rounds AND an irreducible residual review cannot close.
Undecidability -> wide DEFER shrinks coverage, NOT the TCB: the full renderer-bug blast radius applies to 100% of passes, so review cost cannot be amortized by 'it rarely fires.'

The renderer emits Lean source to a kernel subprocess: same injection/laundering surface walnut.py hardened (round 2), plus axiom/sorry/unsafe-`set_option`/import laundering — the bridge re-check must reject these or a 'kernel-checked' proof is vacuous.

Zero trust value until the renderer is proven faithful; given M2 0/24 and 'GREEN != discovers', a non-Q.E.D. measurement tier (zero added TCB) is the lower-trust-surface first move and the renderer should be gated on blind-panel novelty confirmation.
dependencies: A claim->Lean-Prop RENDERER (the new trusted artifact). This is THE deliverable; nothing else is on the critical path for trust.

The proof-side bridge must route through the EXISTING LeanVerifier.discharge / LeanBackend.check_proof so kernel_verified stays single-sourced and producer stays KERNEL_PRODUCER (trust.py:30, validate_edge lines 64-69). If a kernel-bridge backend instead earns the FAITHFULNESS edge, it reuses the ADR 0037 seam as-is (cert.kind='kernel-bridge', a Lean-kernel re-checker registered in FaithfulnessGate.recheckers) — already wired, byte-identical invariants.

An adversarial-soundness-review cycle (ADR 0021/0030 precedent) scoped specifically at the renderer + the bridge re-check's rejection of axiom/sorry/unsafe-option/import Lean — the renderer's own injection allow-list, mirroring walnut.py _safe_walnut_inputs.

A per-backend guard test (the ADR 0037 §3 'each new backend ships with its own guard test' rule, and the M2 'a new path is unguarded, not safe' lesson): PASS implies a kernel-re-checked bridge proof of axiom-free Lean; DEFER never becomes PASS; a renderered-but-unproven Prop never stamps an edge.
Lean 4.31 kernel via Docker (already present) for the bridge re-check; proposal models / ADR 0029 repair panel (already present) to SYNTHESIZE bridge proofs — proposal-side, untrusted, fine.
assessments.rationale:
CAN it be done WITHOUT weakening the invariants? Yes, cleanly — and that is the strongest finding. The three pinned invariants (kernel_verified only in discharge; validate_path requires a KERNEL-producer PROOF edge + a FAITHFULNESS edge; test_invariants.py byte-identical) are ALL preserved if the bridge proof is a genuine Lean proof term checked by the existing LeanBackend.check_proof. Q.E.D. stays honest precisely because the bridge IS a real kernel proof: discharge sets kernel_verified, stamps PROOF_EDGE with producer==KERNEL_PRODUCER, validate_edge (trust.py:64-77) already rejects any non-kernel producer or non-MECHANICAL tier on the proof edge. No edit to trust.py, no edit to test_invariants.py. The seam (sound_backends.py) was even built anticipating 'kernel-bridge' as a certificate kind (Certificate.kind comment line 62; CertificateRechecker doc lines 42-47), so the faithfulness-edge variant is drop-in. So the trust BOUNDARY does not move — what moves is the trust SURFACE (the TCB membership): the renderer joins it as a new load-bearing trusted artifact, in the same TCB slot as Z3 and the Walnut renderer, but with a sharper edge because its failure mode is a permanent, maximally-authoritative kernel-stamped mis-statement.

Why effort L (not M, not XL): the invariant-preservation work is genuinely small and the seam exists, but the renderer is a from-scratch trusted compiler from claim contracts to Lean Props for an unbounded class (automatic-sequence theorems over all n) — that is real, careful, semantics-bearing code, plus a Walnut-style injection allow-list, plus a >3-round adversarial review whose residual cannot be fully closed mechanically. Not XL because it reuses discharge, the Docker kernel, the proposal/repair panel, and the ADR 0037 re-checker registry rather than inventing trust machinery.

Why EASIER NOW (timing): three concrete enablers all exist today and would have to be rebuilt later — (i) the SoundFaithfulnessBackend seam + gate-owned re-checker registry already model 'kernel-bridge' as a first-class kind; (ii) a concrete, narrow first target class (the 12/12 Walnut-reachable automatic-sequence theorems) gives the renderer a bounded, testable domain instead of 'general claims'; (iii) the Walnut soundness review (walnut.py) established the exact adversarial playbook — universality/completeness, freshness/stale-file defense, injection allow-list erring to DEFER — that the renderer review must repeat, so the reviewers are warm and the failure taxonomy is written down. The hard part (proving the renderer faithful) is intrinsic and timing-independent, but the SCAFFOLDING is cheapest now.

The honest caveat for the gating decision: the trust-surface cost is moderate and controllable, BUT it buys nothing until the renderer is proven faithful, and that proof is the one thing the kernel cannot give you. Given M2's 0/24 and the probe's explicit 'GREEN means worth building, not Walnut discovers' bound, standing up a separate NON-Q.E.D. Walnut-decided tier first to MEASURE blind-novelty yield is the lower-trust-surface move: it adds ZERO TCB (no renderer, no proof edge — just a quarantined/tagged tier) and defers the renderer's permanent-mis-statement risk until there is evidence the discovery is real. Build the kernel bridge when (and only when) the blind panel confirms Walnut yields blind-novel results — the renderer's trust cost is justified by landing real discoveries in the Codex, not by the capability to do so.

adjudication.dominant_cost: The claim->Lean-Prop renderer as a new load-bearing TRUSTED artifact with no mechanical re-checker (a renderer bug yields a kernel-checked Q.E.D. of a mis-statement) AND a near-total bridge-synthesis DEFER on the valuable goal shape — these two intrinsic, timing-invariant costs, not the seam plumbing, are the binding constraint.
adjudication.recommended_sequence: 1) Build a separate NON-Q.E.D. Walnut-decided tier first (~S-M, ZERO new TCB): wire the OFF-BY-DEFAULT WalnutBackend into a live path that emits a DECIDED-but-not-promulgated record by reusing Propositio.quarantine/FinishReason + the Calculemus reading-room. 2) Run the unchanged blind human novelty panel (ADR 0034 sec 5) on that output. 3) ONLY if the panel confirms >=1 blind-novel result, run a dedicated bridge-synthesis reachability micro-probe (ADR 0037 sec 4 run-rung discipline) scoped to the automatic-sequence class. 4) ONLY if that probe beats the on-paper RED, build the kernel bridge behind an operator-signed ADR amendment authorizing renderer promotion into the proof-edge TCB, using FORMAL-FIRST publication + a restricted typed per-class grammar + a round-trip/shared-IR decider<->renderer agreement check + a standing known-mis-statement adversarial fixture set.
adjudication.build_trigger: Two conjunctive gates, both must fire: (1) the cheap non-Q.E.D. Walnut tier yields at least one blind-panel-confirmed blind-novel decision over unbounded n (converting "paradigm worth building" into "discovery worth minting Q.E.D. for"); AND (2) a dedicated bridge-synthesis reachability micro-probe returns better than the on-paper RED — i.e. it confirms the target class renders into a Mathlib Prop with a kernel-re-checkable bridge proof for a non-trivial (not 0-2/12) fraction, with wide DEFER expected and acceptable. Until both fire, do not spend the project's scarcest resource (trust-critical review bandwidth) building it.
adjudication.rationale: Adjudication across the five witnesses converges cleanly because they agree on the facts and split only on which cost dominates — and the source confirms the facts. The effort is XL, not the M/L some lenses report, because those lower figures (seam-leverage M, trust-surface L) are scoped to the INTEGRATION/invariant-preservation work alone, which the merged ADR 0037 seam genuinely makes cheap and cheaper-now: the Certificate dataclass already names 'kernel-bridge' as a kind, validate_edge already pins the proof edge to KERNEL_PRODUCER so the bridge can earn honest Q.E.D. with NO edit to trust.py or test_invariants.py, and LeanVerifier.discharge IS already a kernel re-check the bridge wraps rather than rebuilds. But the whole-bridge cost is dominated by the two intrinsic parts the seam touches NEITHER, and both renderer-tcb and bridge-synthesis correctly rate those L/XL. The net easier-now-or-later verdict is LATER: the warm-context discount applies only to the cheap scaffolding (part a), never to the renderer's soundness (a property of the artifact, identical whenever built) or to undecidable synthesis (a fixed mathematical fact, part b) — a textbook intrinsic-cost trap where 'build now' saves a small amount on the part that was never the constraint. On reachability — the question's crux — the first target class is NOT reachable through a useful bridge: the bridge has two goal shapes in inverse value/reachability relation. Shape A (claim_prop<->statement, closable by rfl/simp/decide) is reachable but trivial-by-construction and certifies the renderer, not a theorem — low value, maximal trust cost. Shape B (a full kernel proof of the unbounded-forall theorem) is the valuable target and is exactly (i) the class M2 found 0/24 expressible in the box, (ii) reached by Walnut only via a Buchi-Bruyere automaton-synthesis decision the Lean kernel has no analogue of, and (iii) unsupported by Mathlib (no Thue-Morse/Tribonacci/Stern/power-freeness/Buchi-Bruyere; only a bare catalan). So the bridge does not translate Walnut's decision — it demands a from-scratch re-proof of precisely what Walnut was adopted to avoid, and SOTA provers (Goedel-V2/DeepSeek/Aristotle) DEFER on research combinatorics-on-words. Realistic shape-B DEFER: ~10-12/12. Hence first_target_reachable=false: the bridge would DEFER on ~everything that matters and produce 0-2 textbook closures, the same floor M1 already identified. Weighing measure-before-build against the operator's 'needed eventually for eccentric maths' note: both are honored. The discipline is decisive here because the prior arc (ADR 0034/0035/0036, all 0 blind-novel) is a documented pattern of 'widen X, hope novelty follows' failing, and the Walnut probe is explicitly GREEN on reachability/soundness but 0 on novelty ('GREEN means worth building, not Walnut discovers'). The bridge IS the right eventual mechanism for Q.E.D. on eccentric maths — that is not in dispute — but building the project's hardest, most trust-critical rung to mint Q.E.D. for a discovery channel not yet shown to discover anything inverts the order the project's own evidence demands. The cheap measurement tier de-risks rather than wastes the bridge: nothing in it is throwaway (seam, backend, reading-room all reused unchanged), and it defers the one permanent, unfalsifiable risk — a kernel-stamped mis-statement — until evidence justifies it.
caveats: The seam discount is real but asymmetric: M/L integration effort must NOT be read as M/L on the whole bridge. The renderer (L) and synthesis (XL) are separate and are where 100% of the trust-critical risk lives.

The renderer's faithfulness is the one property the kernel can NEVER certify (it checks proof<->Prop, never Prop<->Enuntiatio). It joins the proof-edge TCB with no mechanical re-checker — unlike every other certificate in the ADR 0037 sec 2 table — so its residual review burden is permanent and unfalsifiable; expect more than Walnut's 3 rounds.

If the bridge is ever built, FORMAL-FIRST publication is the single highest-leverage mitigation: publish the Lean Prop+proof as the primary artifact so a reviewer reads the actual proved statement, making mis-statement visible rather than hidden behind the informal claim.

Decider<->renderer drift is a distinct attack from renderer bugs: the Walnut predicate and the Lean Prop are independent renderings and can denote different statements; a round-trip/shared-IR agreement check is required, not optional.

The strongest-novelty targets (Motzkin mod 8, Gessel/Apery mod 8, odd-Catalan residue) DEFER even in Walnut on engineering grounds (need custom Rowland-Yassawi DFAOs), so for those there is no decided certificate to bridge FROM — out of reach at both layers.

Walnut's live run is currently sandbox-blocked pending operator authorization to run external Java, so there are zero live Walnut decisions today — there is literally nothing to bridge yet, which independently forces the measurement tier first.

Vacuous/weakened-Prop laundering (dropped bound, rendered negation, True) would earn a REAL proof under the bridge — the ADR 0020/0030 vacuous-PASS failure mode migrated onto the proof edge. The known-mis-statement fixture set must be a standing regression that fails to promote.

The reachability verdict hinges on a decision ADR 0037 does not pin: which goal shape the bridge targets. The recommendation assumes the valuable shape B; if the team only ever targets shape A, the bridge is cheaper but certifies nothing and the renderer risk is pure downside.
